  3 ## [1/24] Invitation: Review rOOH Ad Server Tender requirements for working lvl... @ Mon Apr 20, 2026 3pm - 4pm (SGT) (Michael Bui)
  4 Source: gmail | Thread: 19d8f4ed12e58cc0 | Relevance: 8/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-15T04:03:00+00:00 | Labels: ad-server-tender, retail-media-review, vendor-approval, procurement-process, architecture-signoff | Senders: Yi Hao Tan | People: Yi Hao Tan, Allen Umali
  5 Michael Bui is explicitly invited to a working-level review meeting on April 20, 2026, at 3 PM SGT. The session, organized by Yi Hao Tan, focuses on reviewing the requirements for the rOOH (Retail Out-of-Home) Ad Server tender document. Michael's participation is critical to provide sign-off on these technical specifications from an engineering perspective before they are escalated to senior leadership for final approval. This aligns directly with his mandate in Retail Media/Ad Technology and Engineering Governance, specifically regarding vendor selection, solution architecture validation, and procurement processes for the ad server platform.

  9 Madhuri Nalamothu has escalated a critical production defect regarding missing 'BreaktimeEssentials' banners in the Retail Media product catalog on Jira ticket RM-660. The issue affects both 'Tea and Snacks' and 'Drinks and Snacks' categories for guest and logged-in users. Initial testing performed on October 10, 2025, confirmed the banner failure. As of April 15, 2026, the issue remains unresolved with associated test cases failing; consequently, Madhuri has disabled the tests to prevent further pipeline noise and explicitly requested your intervention. Given your mandate over Retail Media engineering governance and production reliability, this requires immediate technical review and root cause analysis.

The discussion involves clarifying truncation logic for the Light Recommender API: specifically, that requests exceeding the default limit will return only up to the default amount (20 products), while explicitly specified sizes allow returning up to that number. Michael previously commented to clarify this behavior. Benji Thian Chan responded to confirm the default size is 20. While Michael has direct involvement via a previous comment and requires awareness of the API specification changes for governance, the email itself is an informational system alert rather than a new action item or critical escalation.

The issue was reported by Madhuri Nalamothu on Jan 5, 2026, with High priority. Michael actively engaged in troubleshooting: he initially disputed the finding on Jan 6 (providing a screenshot showing the ad displayed) and later assisted the QE team in verification on Jan 14 by providing a screen recording of the test account behavior.  87 Michael Bui is a direct participant in Jira ticket DPD-838 regarding the transition to impression-based inventory and multi-banner delivery. The ticket has moved from 'To Be Defined' to 'In Development' as of April 17th. Chee Hoe Leong split and deployed the feature flag `be_osmos_multi_banners_enabled` following UAT sign-off received from business on April 16th, confirmed by Nikhil Grover who instructed proceeding with deployment with the flag off.

The logic change shifts endemic content prioritization and deduplication to the ad unit (AU) level, impacting fallback behaviors and sequence logic across Home, Search, and Category pages. As this introduces new multi-banner serving mechanics (2-3 ads per AU), Michael's technical judgment is required to validate that these scope changes do not breach backend architecture standards or SLO targets. Manual E2E testing for complex visual scenarios is now pending, a phase where his oversight on production reliability is critical.

 99 ## [22/24] Issue with Missing UBID
100 Source: gmail | Thread: 19d52fdf13c8b0bd | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-17T10:22:00+00:00 | Labels: retail-media, data-integrity, vendor-escalation, architecture-review, slo-compliance | Senders: Rahul, me | People: Rahul Jain, Nikhil Grover, Michael Bui
101 On April 17, 2026, vendor Rahul Jain (Osmos) sent a direct follow-up to Michael Bui and Nikhil Grover regarding the 'Missing UBID' incident. This escalates a critical data integrity issue in the Retail Media pipeline affecting Product Listing Ads (PLA) and Display units, where User Browsing IDs were initially flagged as missing. While Daryl Ng previously suggested that CLI UBIDs in UUID format may represent guest users rather than actual data loss, Michael had requested clarification on the real business impact. As the Architect/Lead for Engineering Governance, Michael must now determine if this discrepancy stems from a configuration error or expected behavior to restore service reliability and SLO compliance. The vendor's inquiry demands a definitive technical assessment or architectural correction decision.
102 
103 ## [23/24] Proposed Approach for SKU Handling Across Old & New App
104 Source: gmail | Thread: 19d9b2b3ecdbf46d | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-17T11:19:00+00:00 | Labels: ad-technology, sku-mapping, vendor-proposal, architecture-review, retail-media | Senders: Rahul Jain | People: Rahul Jain, Nikhil
105 Rahul Jain (Osmos) proposes a technical approach to handle SKU mapping between FPG's old and new mobile apps during their coexistence period. The solution involves passing the new SKU in custom labels within OSMOS to enable centralized mapping, allowing ads to be served across both apps without duplicating products or requiring changes to the legacy app. As Michael Bui is explicitly addressed regarding this architecture proposal for Retail Media/Ad Technology, his technical judgment is required. This aligns with his domain expertise in ad technology and platform architecture, specifically concerning service integrations and vendor communications that require engineering input.
106 
107 ## [24/24] Issue with the event segment audience pipeline.
108 Source: gmail | Thread: 19ce6ebdc3530d4d | Relevance: 9/10 | Mention: direct | Updated: 2026-04-17T11:22:00+00:00 | Labels: production-migration, architecture-governance, reliability-improvement, audience-sync, vendor-coordination | Senders: Vipul | People: Rahul Jain, Michael, Nikhil
109 Rahul Jain from Osmos sent a direct follow-up on April 17 regarding the critical migration of the event segment audience pipeline. This architectural shift moves from an API-based sync to a PubSub mechanism to resolve autoscaling lag and 5XX errors observed during the March 13 traffic spike. As the engineer governing backend standards and production reliability for this domain, Michael must act immediately to ensure all existing audiences currently pointing to 'OSMOS PROD Audiences Sync Function (Fairprice)' are remapped to the new destination function, "Osmos : Google Cloud PubSub [Prod + Stag]". The older function is scheduled for deprecation post-migration. This task requires Michael's oversight to validate that teams adhere to the new standard and confirm the transition mitigates real-time processing bottlenecks affecting SLOs.
